name: Jack Crystal
description: American jazz promoter and record dealer (1909-1963)

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: College Jazz Goes to Carnegie Hall
Midnight Performance: College Jazz Goes to Carnegie Hall

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: There were two guys who ran sessions there: Bob Maltz and Jack Crystal. Both of them died relatively young. Jack Crystal is famous for being Billy Crystal’s father and Milt Gabler’s brother-in-law.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Schaap
about_quotes.evidence: I knew Jack Crystal and Bernhardt Crystal... Jack Crystal used to let me into the Central Plaza for cheap... Jack Crystal was nice to me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Schaap interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: Bob Maltz and Jack Crystal, who is Billy Crystal’s father, they booked musicians that were available for relatively little.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 9, 2013

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: The other one was run by a guy named Jack Crystal... Jack Crystal was . . . He had the Commodore Record Shop
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Lovett
about_quotes.evidence: I saw Bill a few months ago in New York at a benefit at the Central Plaza for Jack Crystal.

about_quotes.speaker: Johnny Varro
about_quotes.evidence: One was run by Bob Maltz and the other was run by Jack Crystal, who is Billy Crystal’s dad.
about_quotes.doc_title: Johnny Varro interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001
